scope: Computational falsification of exact proof objects for non-negative weighted-sum supportability of one selected candidate

candidate_generation.small_random_instances: One epsilon-constraint candidate and one SHA-256 ordered feasible control are selected before supportability classification
candidate_generation.controlled_instances: Candidate and challengers are fixed algebraically before certificate generation
prohibited: Using a finite weight grid as ground truth
Selecting random-instance candidates from certificate output
Deleting failed cases after observing results

baselines: Independent exact support-polytope vertex enumeration over the complete nondominated image
Exact analytic support interval for p=2
Fixed weight grids with denominators 100, 1000 and 10000

research_questions.question: Does the generator agree with independent exact complete-image classification?
research_questions.acceptance: Complete agreement and no checker rejection on the fixed correctness corpus

research_questions.question: Are all emitted negative certificates sparse and exact?
research_questions.acceptance: Every negative object has between one and p atoms and passes independent replay

research_questions.question: Is the p-atom bound sharp in controlled dimensions?
research_questions.acceptance: Generated tight cases attain p atoms for p=2,...,5 and the direct construction is verified through p=8

research_questions.question: Can a fixed weight grid miss an exactly supportable candidate?
research_questions.acceptance: At least one exact false negative for each declared grid

research_questions.question: Does local checking reduce audit cost on large negative cases?
research_questions.acceptance: Median in-process checker time is below generation time in every timed large negative case

research_questions.question: Are the trust boundary and mutation defences explicit?
research_questions.acceptance: No P2 object is reported as P1 and all 24 resealed semantic mutations are rejected

timing.thread_policy: single Python process with no parallel experimental execution

interpretive_limits: Controlled large negative cases are theorem-targeted constructions and not prevalence samples
Positive binary-linear certificates remain P2 under the current checker contract
Atom count is not a universal byte-size bound
Single-environment timings are descriptive rather than a portable performance ranking
The study concerns weighted-sum supportability and not completeness of nonlinear scalarisations
